title: A child's schoolbag
premise: A small backpack, still packed, no child.

stages.narration: A small backpack sits upright against a step, zipped, a cartoon dinosaur worn pale on the flap. Homework in a folder, a juice box gone sticky beside it. No child. You make yourself stop looking for one.

choices.label: Set it somewhere it can be seen, and go
Check it for anything you could use

effects.note: went through the child's bag; took nothing

notes: city-voices, a moral micro-beat feeding Humanity. Looking turns up nothing worth the cost of having looked; the honouring gesture costs time; walking on is free but doesn't leave you. Human-only (the child is gone, human).
